props.name: ariaControls
props.description:
Adds `aria-controls` attribute to the component.
If the component controls any secondary content (for example, another form field), use this to provide an ID referring to the secondary content.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description:
Adds an `aria-label` to the native control.

Use this if you don't have a visible label for this control.

props.name: ariaRequired
props.description: Specifies whether to add `aria-required` to the native control.

props.name: checked
props.description: Specifies if the component is selected.

props.name: controlId
props.description: Specifies the ID of the native form element. By default, it uses an automatically generated ID.

props.name: disabled
props.description:
Specifies if the control is disabled, which prevents the
user from modifying the value and prevents the value from
being included in a form submission. A disabled control can't
receive focus.

props.name: indeterminate
props.description:
Specifies that the component is in an indeterminate state. The behavior of this property replicates
the behavior of [the respective property](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/checkbox#Indeterminate_state_checkboxes)
in the native control.

props.name: name
props.description: Specifies the name of the control used in HTML forms.

props.name: readOnly
props.description:
Specifies if the control is read-only, which prevents the
user from modifying the value. Should be used only inside forms.
A read-only control is still focusable.
If both `readOnly` and `disabled` are set, `disabled` takes precedence.

slots.name: default
slots.description: The control's label that's displayed next to the checkbox. A state change occurs when a user clicks on it.

slots.name: description
slots.description: Description that appears below the label.

events.name: blur
events.description: Called when input focus is removed from the UI control.

events.name: change
events.description: Called when the user changes the component state. The event `detail` contains the current value for the `checked` property.

events.name: focus
events.description: Called when input focus is moved to the UI control.
